article_title: MIT Spinout 1s1 Energy Develops Boron-Based Membrane That Cuts Green Hydrogen Electrolyzer Energy Use by 30 Percent

content_preview.title: MIT Spinout 1s1 Energy Develops Boron-Based Membrane That Cuts Green Hydrogen Electrolyzer Energy Use by 30 Percent
content_preview.summary: The startup's chemically tailored boron polymer membranes already achieve the U.S. Department of Energy's 2031 efficiency target of 77 percent for proton exchange membrane electrolysis, while partner tests show a 60 percent reduction in operating costs.
content_preview.body_excerpt:
## Overview

1s1 Energy, a startup spun out of research by MIT-trained engineers, has developed a new class of proton exchange membranes built on boron-based chemistry that reduce the energy required to produce green hydrogen by approximately 30 percent. In partner testing, electrolyzers fitted with the company's membranes needed only 70 percent of the energy consumed by incumbent devices to produce each kilogram of hydrogen, [according to MIT News](https://news.mit.edu/2026/toward-cheaper-clean...

editor_notes.content_quality: Strong hydrogen energy article with clear technical detail.
editor_notes.source_verification: MIT News: VERIFIED — 1s1 Energy, boron membrane, 30% energy reduction, 60% cost reduction, DOE 2031 target met early, Dan Sobek co-founder. DOE and CleanTechnica in source snapshots.
editor_notes.factual_accuracy: All claims verified: energy reduction, DOE targets, cost projections.
editor_notes.tone_assessment: Neutral. Covers both potential and uncertainties.
editor_notes.originality: No prior green hydrogen electrolyzer coverage.
